description: Fix two missing error handling gaps in the Chrome extension: uncaught atob() exception in network capture and unhandled JSON parse failure in the offscreen ws:setUrl handler.

userStories.title: Add try/catch around atob() in network capture loadingFinished
userStories.description: As a developer using browser network capture tools, I want invalid base64 response bodies to be handled gracefully so that the capture pipeline does not throw an unhandled exception.
acceptanceCriteria: atob() call in the Network.loadingFinished callback is wrapped in a try/catch
On decode failure, the response body field is set to a descriptive error string (e.g., '[base64 decode failed]') instead of crashing
A console.warn is emitted with the requestId for debuggability
All other base64-encoded responses continue to decode correctly (existing behavior preserved)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts, around lines 305-312.

The Network.loadingFinished callback calls `getResponseBody` and then decodes the body:

```ts
const body = responseData.base64Encoded
  ? new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(responseData.body), c => c.charCodeAt(0)))
  : responseData.body;
```

`atob()` throws a `DOMException` if the input contains characters outside the base64 alphabet. Every other failure mode in this callback is already guarded: `chrome.runtime.lastError`, missing result, missing body, evicted requests (via `pendingRequests` map lookup), and binary MIME type filtering. This is the one gap.

The fix is straightforward: wrap the base64 decode path in a try/catch. On failure, set body to a placeholder string like `'[base64 decode failed]'` and log a warning. Do NOT skip the entire response — still store the entry with the error placeholder so the capture timeline remains complete.

Do not change the non-base64 path. Do not refactor surrounding code.

userStories.title: Add error handling for JSON parse failure in offscreen ws:setUrl handler
userStories.description: As the extension offscreen document, I want the ws:setUrl handler to gracefully handle non-JSON /ws-info responses so that sendResponse is always called and the Chrome message channel does not leak.
acceptanceCriteria: The response.json() call in the ws:setUrl async IIFE is wrapped in a try/catch
On JSON parse failure, sendResponse is called with { ok: false, reason: 'Invalid /ws-info response' } or similar
The handler does not proceed to change mcpServerUrl on parse failure
Valid JSON /ws-info responses continue to work correctly (existing behavior preserved)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts, around lines 417-464 (the `case 'ws:setUrl':` handler).

The handler is an async IIFE `void (async () => { ... })()` that calls `fetchWsInfo(httpBase)` and then parses the response:

```ts
const result = await fetchWsInfo(httpBase);
if ('response' in result && result.response.ok) {
  const wsInfo = (await result.response.json()) as { wsUrl?: string };
```

If the server returns HTTP 200 with a non-JSON body (malformed, HTML error page, empty), `result.response.json()` throws a `SyntaxError`. Because the IIFE is `void`-ed, the rejection is swallowed. `sendResponse()` is never called, and Chrome keeps the message channel open until the sender times out. The URL change silently fails.

The fix: wrap the `result.response.json()` call (and the subsequent logic that uses `wsInfo`) in a try/catch. In the catch, call `sendResponse({ ok: false, reason: '...' })` and return.

Important: the async handler returns `true` at line 463 to tell Chrome to keep the message channel open for async sendResponse. This is correct and must be preserved. The fix must ensure sendResponse is called on ALL code paths within the IIFE, including the new catch.

Do not refactor the overall structure of the handler. Do not change fetchWsInfo. Only add error handling around the JSON parse.
